title: OpenZeppelin API Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary for the OpenZeppelin Web3 security platform, covering Defender, Relayer, Monitor, and Contracts concepts.

terms.definition: A service that manages blockchain transaction submission, nonce management, gas estimation, and retries on behalf of decentralized applications. Supports EVM, Solana, and Stellar networks.

OpenZeppelin's cloud-based developer security platform (sunsetting July 1, 2026) providing auditing, deployment, monitoring, and automation tools for smart contracts. Users are migrating to the open-source Relayer and Monitor.

An open-source blockchain monitoring service that watches for specific on-chain activities (function calls, events, transaction patterns) and triggers notifications via Slack, Discord, Email, Telegram, Webhooks, or custom scripts.
A cryptographic key management component responsible for signing transactions and data. Supports Local (HSM/file), AWS KMS, Google Cloud KMS, Vault (HashiCorp), Turnkey, and CDP signer backends.

Self-executing code deployed on a blockchain network that automatically enforces agreement terms. OpenZeppelin Contracts provides battle-tested, audited Solidity implementations of common contract patterns.
Ethereum token standard defining a fungible token interface with transfer, approval, and allowance mechanisms. Implemented in OpenZeppelin Contracts.
Ethereum non-fungible token (NFT) standard defining unique, non-interchangeable tokens with ownership and transfer mechanics. Implemented in OpenZeppelin Contracts.
Ethereum multi-token standard allowing a single contract to manage multiple fungible and non-fungible token types efficiently. Implemented in OpenZeppelin Contracts.
A blockchain transaction where the end-user does not pay gas fees directly; instead, a relayer or meta-transaction system sponsors the gas costs, improving user experience.
A transaction where gas fees are paid by a third party (sponsor/relayer) rather than the transaction originator. The OpenZeppelin Relayer API supports building and quoting sponsored transactions.
A smart contract design pattern that separates storage from logic, enabling upgradeable contracts. OpenZeppelin Contracts implements transparent proxy, UUPS, and beacon proxy patterns.
A mechanism for defining and enforcing role-based permissions in smart contracts. OpenZeppelin Contracts provides Ownable, AccessControl, and TimelockController implementations.
A security pattern preventing reentrant calls to a function during its execution. OpenZeppelin Contracts provides the ReentrancyGuard modifier to protect against reentrancy attacks.
Classification of blockchain networks supported by OpenZeppelin tools: EVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine compatible chains), Solana, and Stellar.
Ethereum Improvement Proposal 1559 introducing a base fee mechanism for gas pricing with optional priority tips. The OpenZeppelin Relayer supports EIP-1559 transaction pricing.
An extension mechanism in the OpenZeppelin Relayer that adds custom functionality (e.g., Jupiter swap on Solana, transfer operations) via configurable call routing.
JSON Web Token used for authentication in the OpenZeppelin Defender API. Tokens are short-lived and negotiated via SRP protocol through Amazon Cognito.
A formal security assessment of smart contract code produced by OpenZeppelin's security audit team. OpenZeppelin has audited many of the most critical DeFi and Web3 protocols.
